description: The performance of different machine learning frameworks, like the support vector machine (SVM), random forest, gradient descent, and decision tree, is implemented to enhance the performance of the OFDM system model. This study used two contrasting machine learning frameworks to explore how data coverage influences high-latitude Rs modeling and prediction: an Annual Coverage Model (ACM), trained solely on sites with full annual observational records, and a Hybrid Dataset Model (HDM), which integrated seasonally incomplete sites as well.

A high-performance data processing engine built with Rust, designed for modern machine learning workflows. Zi provides a unified framework for data quality assessment, cleaning, transformation, sampling, and augmentation with exceptional speed and reliability.

Developed a hybrid machine learning framework for real-time anomaly detection and intelligent alert optimization in IoMT health monitoring systems. Machine learning framework for diabetes prediction using clinical data. Includes preprocessing, feature engineering, and model comparison (LR, SVM, NN, ensembles). Evaluated with precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC to ensure reliable and interpretable decision support.
